title: Kubernetes Networking and Ingress
learningObjectives: Understand Kubernetes networking model and DNS
Configure Ingress for HTTP routing and TLS termination
Use NetworkPolicies to restrict pod communication
Work with EndpointSlices and headless Services

theory.content:
Kubernetes Networking Model

Every Pod gets a unique IP. All Pods can communicate with all other Pods without NAT. Services get their own stable ClusterIP from the service CIDR range. kube-proxy implements Service routing via iptables/IPVS.

DNS in Kubernetes

CoreDNS provides DNS for Services and Pods. Service DNS: <service>.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local

# Full DNS: my-service.my-namespace.svc.cluster.local
# Short form within same namespace: my-service
# From different namespace: my-service.other-namespace

Ingress

Ingress exposes HTTP/HTTPS routes from outside the cluster to Services. Requires an Ingress Controller (nginx-ingress, Traefik, ALB Ingress).

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: app-ingress
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - app.example.com
      secretName: app-tls-secret
  rules:
    - host: app.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /api
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: api-service
                port:
                  number: 80
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: frontend-service
                port:
                  number: 80

NetworkPolicy

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-api-only
  namespace: production
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: database
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app: api
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 5432

Headless Services

Setting clusterIP: None creates a headless service — DNS returns individual Pod IPs instead of a ClusterIP. Used with StatefulSets for stable network identity.
